what_it_does: Declares a single PLATFORM dictionary that registers Claude Code as an instance row in a Mirror-Registry / PlatformRegistry system, holding all platform-specific constants: CLI flag mappings, capability axes, permission model, invocation binding, discovery sources, and resource governance.
observations: One top-level SPAWN_FLAG_BODY_KEY_MAP maps snake_case body keys to kebab CLI flag names.
PLATFORM is a pure-data dict with no functions or classes; the head_builder callable binding was moved to platforms/_wiring.py to preserve row purity.

Contains executable_locator, invocation_kind, discovery_sources, version_source, signal_sets, consumer_reserved_invariants, invocation_binding, permission_model, tool_surface, tool_server_wiring, resource_governance, and projection_targets.
transport_invariants is a declared fallback set that the registry overwrites with a live derivation from a registered head_builder thunk.
Post-R6 correction removes --mcp-config and --allowedTools from transport invariants and places them on consent capability axes.
--add-dir is classified under the dirs capability axis requiring operator consent, not as a hard transport lock.
Comments cite spec sections, file:line references, and ticket IDs such as F-FIX-2, F-FIX-5, F-FIX-9, R6.
standouts: The SPAWN_FLAG_BODY_KEY_MAP acts as a single source of truth for flag-name foreign keys across the system.
transport_invariants are explicitly a fallback, with live derivation intended to replace them at registry load.
Permission model maps plan / acceptEdits / bypassPermissions and marks the hazard flag --dangerously-skip-permissions.
The file is intentionally data-only so a second platform can copy its shape for generalization.
conventions: Heavy inline spec citations (§X, file:line, ticket IDs).
Separation of snake_case body keys from kebab-case CLI flag names.
Data-only row purity: imports plus one dict, no executable logic.
Comments explain invariants and recent fixes rather than hiding them.
concerns: Large nested dict duplicates flag strings between SPAWN_FLAG_BODY_KEY_MAP and nested sections, creating drift risk if cross-checks are not enforced.
The fallback transport_invariants list could be used if the head_builder thunk is missing, so it must stay synchronized with the live derivation.
Some values such as known_paths are hardcoded and may not match every deployment.
notes: This file is the canonical data row for Claude Code in the platform registry; it owns every Claude-specific string so the engine can remain generic.
questions: Where is platforms/_wiring.py and how exactly is the head_builder thunk registered with the registry?
How does PlatformEntry.model_validate enforce the schema over this dict?
What is the precise semantics of floor_guard: 30 and parse_rule: commander-options-text?
How are projection_targets consumed by the Dynamic Capability Registry?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a pure data row registering Claude Code, and the actual content matches: a single dict with no definitions or classes.
apparent_themes: Row purity and data-driven platform registration.
Separation of platform-specific strings from generic engine code.
Live derivation preferred over static declaration.
intention_signals: Encode the entire Claude Code surface as a declarative, versionable registry row so a generic supervisor/engine can spawn, discover capabilities, enforce posture, and generalize to other platforms without hardcoding Claude specifics.
novelty: The deliberate pure-data row design with callables factored into a separate wiring module, and the explicit R1/R3 classification of flags via a body-key map and capability axes.
contribution: A complete, self-contained data schema instance for a CLI-driven AI coding assistant, including flag vocabulary, capability axes, permission modes, event shapes, and resource limits, usable as a template for registering similar platforms.
summary_for_embedding: platforms/claude_code.py declares the pure-data PLATFORM registry row for Claude Code, mapping body keys to CLI flags, defining transport invariants, capability axes, permission modes, invocation bindings, and discovery sources for a generic Mirror-Registry system.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize every Claude Code-specific configuration string and policy in a single data-only row so the generic engine can spawn and supervise Claude Code without platform-specific hardcoding.
